closed_reason: Typed startup replay recovery contract verified with rebuild and fatal-path regressions
description: Open recovery currently special-cases a narrow replay error class and treats others as fatal. Encode recoverability in error types and require exhaustive recovery handling at startup boundary.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit: /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs jj change kwqryzqv / d75b081b36fe. Added typed StartupRecovery classification for replay-open failures, narrowed rebuilds to clear index-derived replay defects, and added daemon-level regressions proving recoverable sealed-length mismatch rebuilds while fatal WAL corruption preserves the persisted index. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check; just dylint; cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo xtest; cargo test -p beads-daemon-core --all-features startup_recovery -- --nocapture; cargo test -p beads-daemon --lib open_rebuilds_when_catch_up_hits_sealed_segment_len_mismatch -- --nocapture; cargo test -p beads-daemon --lib open_does_not_delete_index_for_fatal_catch_up_corruption -- --nocapture. Adversarial review: Einstein clean after iteration, Huygens clean after iteration.

title: P0: Classify replay errors as recoverable vs terminal in open path

description:
## What's Wrong
The threshold for switching from detailed to count-based commit messages is hardcoded to 5 in to_commit_message().

## Where
- src/git/sync.rs:92 (`if !self.details.is_empty() && total <= 5`)

## Why It Matters
Minor - the magic number 5 isn't configurable or documented. Fine for now, but if users want different behavior they can't change it.

## Suggested Fix
Either document why 5, or make it a const at module level.
title: Smart commit message threshold (5) is hardcoded

description:
**Problem**
State blobs are trusted JSON; corruption is only detected if JSON parsing fails. Silent corruption could lead to inconsistent state or data loss.

**Design**
Include a checksum (e.g., SHA256) in `meta.json` for state/tombstones/deps blobs and verify on read. On mismatch, surface a clear error and avoid overwriting state.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Checksums written on commit
- [ ] Checksums verified on load
- [ ] Corruption returns a structured error

**Files:** src/git/wire.rs, src/git/sync.rs, src/api/mod.rs, SPEC.md
title: Add integrity checksum for store blobs

description:
**Problem**
Need bd pour command to instantiate protos as persistent molecules. Pour is the primary way to create work from templates.

**What is Pour?**
Pour instantiates a proto (template) as a persistent mol:
- Phase: Proto (solid) → Mol (liquid)
- Storage: Creates real issues in .beads/ that sync to git
- Variables: Substitutes {{var}} placeholders with --var flags
- Result: Epic + children with dependencies intact

**How it Works (Go beads)**
1. Load proto (from DB OR cook formula inline)
2. Clone subgraph with variable substitution
3. Apply defaults from formula VarDefs
4. Create all issues + dependencies + labels
5. Schedule autoflush to sync to JSONL

**Command Syntax**
bd pour <proto-or-formula> [--var k=v]... [--assignee agent] [--attach other-proto]

**Examples**
- bd pour mol-feature --var name=auth
- bd pour beads-release --var version=1.0.0
- bd pour mol-epic --var title="My Epic" --assignee claude

**Design**
Implements cloneSubgraph from template.go:
- Variable substitution in title/description/fields
- Preserve dependency graph topology
- Apply formula defaults (VarDef.default)
- Check required variables (VarDef.required)
- Handle attachments (--attach for bonding during pour)

**Files to Study (Go beads)**
- cmd/bd/pour.go - pour command implementation
- cmd/bd/template.go - cloneSubgraph (line ~500)
- cmd/bd/cook.go - resolveAndCookFormula (inline cooking)

**Acceptance**
- [ ] bd pour command implemented
- [ ] Loads proto from DB or cooks formula inline
- [ ] Variable substitution works
- [ ] Defaults applied from formula
- [ ] Required variable validation
- [ ] Creates issues + deps + labels
- [ ] --assignee flag works
- [ ] Tests for pour workflow
title: Add bd pour command to beads-rs
